name: Suspicious Userinit Child Process
description: Detection of a suspicious child process of `userinit.exe`. `userinit.exe` is a legitimate Windows process that is responsible for launching the user's shell (typically `explorer.exe`). A malicious child process of `userinit.exe` is a strong indicator of an adversary attempting to bypass security defenses and achieve persistence.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered on the host **{host}**: a suspicious process, **{image}**, was launched by **{parent_image}** (userinit.exe). This is a strong indicator of **Masquerading** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1036**) and an attempt at **Persistence**. `userinit.exe` should only launch `explorer.exe` or legitimate logon scripts. The command line was **{command}**, and the event occurred under the user **{user}**. Immediate investigation is required to determine the legitimacy of this action and to remove the malicious process.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The name of the host where the event occurred.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: The name of the suspicious child process.

parameters.name: parent_image
parameters.description: The name of the parent process, which is expected to be 'userinit.exe'.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: The full command line of the suspicious process.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account that performed the action.
